topic: Two-zone disc topology — V13 cross-equatorial fit
change_summary: detail_verdict_length grew from 235 to 1110 (+372%); a post-hoc-test rider was appended to the verdict text. Sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-05T20:52Z; obe_skipped=7 (WIN-050/028/009/036/060/018/059 already re-reviewed since audit). Drift-audit fresh at 53.7h.
our_argument_summary: WIN-057 (verdict: Misleading) attacks the V13 two-zone disc topology on three grounds: (1) the equatorial ring radius 14,105 km is a free parameter fitted to six known city-pair distances (curve-fit, not prediction); (2) NH RMSE regressed from 5.2% to 7.3% while cross-equatorial improved from 25–78% to 6.2%; (3) the 6.2% RMSE result remains ~600× worse than standard geodesy (Vincenty/WGS84 sub-0.01% with two parameters) and silently excludes near-equatorial cities below ~12° latitude (OPEN-015). The c2→c3 drift is the addition of a generic post-hoc-test rider in detail_verdict_text saying the value 'derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters'.

relevant_files: raw-text/monitor.py (no monitoring entry for this WIN per code_analysis tag)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: V13 dome introduces two-zone topology with an additional fitted parameter (equatorial ring radius = 14,105 km) added on top of the existing H₀, λ in H(r)=H₀·exp(−r/λ), and EW angular scale 0.9941. The fitting is to six cross-equatorial city-pair distances from WGS84 published values. The dome's monitor.py has no live-fetch domain or hardcoded comparison for this WIN; the 'validation' is documented in static prose rather than a running script.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: monitoring=none (correctly tagged). The WIN is a static topology claim rather than a per-domain prediction with a tolerance band.
data_sources_used: Six known WGS84 city-pair distances chosen as fit targets
OPEN-015 published acknowledgment of equatorial exclusion zone
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: OPEN-015 explicitly excludes cities within ~12° of equator from the model's domain — this is an admission that the V13 topology cannot handle a substantial slice of the surface. The dome reports no out-of-sample validation (short routes, equator-to-NH routes, SH-internal routes beyond the showcase six).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None on the monitoring side — code_analysis.monitoring='none' is correct (no fetch logic in monitor.py). However, code_analysis.derives_from_dome='true' contradicts the detail_evidence text which says the 14,105 km value is 'fitted to minimize residuals on six known city-pair distances, not derived from dome geometry.' See holes_found below.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py has no domain entry for V13 two-zone topology. The claim is a static prose+table comparison, not a running prediction harness. Correctly tagged.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics does not predict a two-zone equatorial-ring topology with a separate ring radius parameter; the dome's V13 topology has no globe-physics analog (the globe gives a single ellipsoid with two parameters). Correctly tagged false.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Six known WGS84 city-pair distances are public, indexed, and were used as the fit targets (the 'observation' that the V13 'prediction' matches). The dome's V13 release post-dates the existence of those distances. Correctly tagged.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: TAG IS QUESTIONABLE AS WRITTEN. The detail_evidence text directly says the 14,105 km value is 'fitted to minimize residuals on six known city-pair distances, NOT derived from dome geometry.' The two-zone topological framework is dome-derived, but the load-bearing numerical parameter is fit. Compare WIN-069 (same detail_group) which carries the nuanced explanation 'V13 topology + refractive profile are dome-derived; numerical parameters are fit to globe data, but the geometric framework originates from dome model structure.' WIN-057 either (a) needs that same nuance, or (b) should flip to derives_from_dome=false (matching WIN-027 in the same detail_group). As-is, the bare 'true' contradicts the WIN's own evidence and is exploitable by a defender.

holes_found.description: Self-contradiction between detail_verdict_text and detail_evidence on whether the 14,105 km parameter is fitted vs derived. The post-hoc-test rider added to detail_verdict_text says 'The numerical value is one the dome framework derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters (disc radius, firmament height, chosen symmetry axis, or similar)'. The detail_evidence says exactly the opposite: 'equatorial ring radius = 14,105 km — a value fitted to minimize residuals on six known city-pair distances, not derived from dome geometry.' A defender can quote our evidence text verbatim against our verdict text and accuse the review of self-contradiction. This rider was added by the change that triggered this re-review (c2→c3 detail_verdict_length +372%) and appears to be a generic post-hoc template that was not adapted to WIN-057's actual fact pattern.
holes_found.recommendation: Edit the post-hoc rider in WIN-057's detail_verdict_text to match the actual fact pattern. Suggested replacement: 'The 14,105 km equatorial ring radius is a free parameter fitted to the same six city-pair distances used to validate the model; the V13 topological framework is dome-stipulated, but the numerical parameter that does the work is curve-fit to its own validation set, which is the post-hoc problem in its strongest form — there is no out-of-sample prediction.' This formulation matches the actual evidence text and is more damaging to the dome than the generic template.

holes_found.description: code_analysis.derives_from_dome inconsistency across detail_group WIN-027/057/069. Same group, same topic (disc-vs-globe coordinate fitting): WIN-027 has derives_from_dome=false, WIN-057 has true, WIN-069 has true with an explicit explanation field noting numerical parameters are fit to globe data. WIN-057's bare 'true' lacks the WIN-069 nuance and contradicts its own evidence text (see major hole above).
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) flip WIN-057's derives_from_dome to false (matching WIN-027 and matching the evidence text's 'not derived from dome geometry'), or (b) keep true and add a derives_from_dome_evidence field copying WIN-069's nuanced explanation. Option (a) is simpler and more honest. Option (b) preserves the framework-vs-parameter distinction. Either resolution removes the cross-WIN inconsistency and the within-WIN contradiction in one move.

holes_found.description: Population claim in detail_extra appears overstated by roughly 2×. The text says 'those within approximately 12° of the equator. This exclusion affects roughly 40% of the world's population, including Singapore (1.4°N), Bogotá (4.7°N), Nairobi (1.3°S), and Kuala Lumpur (3.1°N).' The ±12° latitude band actually contains roughly 17–22% of world population (Kummu & Varis 2011 type estimates from gridded population datasets). The full tropical band ±23.5° (Tropic of Cancer to Tropic of Capricorn) holds ~40% — that is the figure that may have been inadvertently substituted. The four named cities are all within ±5° so they do not anchor the 40% figure.
holes_found.recommendation: Change 40% of the world's population to a defensible figure such as 'roughly 1.5 billion people (about 18-20% of the world's population)' or rewrite to 'roughly two billion people' with the 12° framing. Optionally cross-check against a current gridded population product if precision matters. The argument does not need 40% to land — even 18% is a damning systematic exclusion zone for a 'general geometric framework.'

holes_found.description: The post-hoc rider's bracketed parenthetical lists 'disc radius, firmament height, chosen symmetry axis, or similar' as the dome-geometric parameters. None of those is the 14,105 km equatorial ring radius — it is an additional fitted parameter, distinct from disc_radius (20,015 km), firmament_height (9,086 km), or any symmetry axis. The list reads as generic boilerplate that doesn't fit this WIN. Tied to the major self-contradiction above; once the rider is rewritten to match the actual fact pattern, this resolves automatically.
holes_found.recommendation: Drop the parenthetical disc-radius/firmament-height/symmetry-axis list when rewriting the rider. Replace with explicit naming of the 14,105 km equatorial ring radius and the H₀, λ, EW angular scale parameters that actually drive the V13 fit.

stronger_arguments.description: The cleanest single-sentence demolition of WIN-057 is the in-sample/out-of-sample asymmetry: WGS84's two parameters (a, f) generalize to billions of points worldwide at sub-cm accuracy with no exclusion zone; the dome's 14,105 km parameter is fit to six routes and validated only on those same six routes, with a region of total failure (OPEN-015) carved out of the domain. This is more damaging than 'curve-fitting' alone because it explicitly shows the validation gap. Suggest one sentence in detail_verdict_text: 'WGS84 generalizes to sub-centimeter accuracy on points outside the calibration set; the dome's 14,105 km has been validated only on the six routes used to fit it, with a region of explicit non-applicability around the equator.'
sources: OPEN-015 dome-side acknowledgment of <12° exclusion zone

stronger_arguments.description: Information-criterion framing. AIC/BIC penalize free parameters; a model with 4+ free parameters fitted to 6 data points has effectively zero predictive degrees of freedom. Under AIC, V13 vs V12 with a fitted parameter on the same six-route validation set is structurally a worse model unless the residual sum of squares falls by more than the parameter penalty. This is the dome-defender's preferred academic framing, and we should anticipate it. Suggested addition: 'Under AIC/BIC scoring, adding a parameter to fit the same six validation points incurs a complexity penalty that the 25-78% → 6.2% RMSE drop does not necessarily overcome on a per-route basis.'

kernel_of_truth.description: There is a real model-improvement claim that deserves credit: V13 produces materially better fit than V12 on the six cross-equatorial routes (25–78% → 6.2% RMSE — see data/wins.json:WIN-057 detail_evidence). That is not zero; iterating a model to fit observations is a normal scientific activity and dome V13 did demonstrably reduce residuals on the routes it was fit to. The detail_evidence acknowledges this with 'The improvement from 25–78% to 6.2% RMSE is real but expected'.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The improvement is in-sample on the same six routes used to derive the parameter, not out-of-sample on novel data; NH accuracy regressed (5.2% → 7.3% per data/wins.json:WIN-057 detail_evidence) so the V13 'win' bought one region's accuracy by losing another's; the absolute level (6.2% RMSE, ~400 km on a 6500 km route) is still ~600× worse than Vincenty/WGS84 sub-0.01% (verified: 6.2/0.01=620, see Quantitative Verification below); and the dome explicitly excludes near-equatorial cities (OPEN-015 cited in data/wins.json:WIN-057 detail_evidence and detail_extra). The kernel of 'V13 fits better than V12 on the chosen routes' is real; it just does not survive the in-sample/out-of-sample asymmetry, the NH regression, the 600× absolute-accuracy gap, or the equatorial exclusion zone.

advocate_mode.best_defense: I am a fair-minded dome defender who has read the review's six methodological principles. I would attack WIN-057 like this: the reviewers concede the V13 model is a real improvement on V12. They concede that adding parameters to a model in response to data is iterative refinement — every science does this. WGS84 itself was iteratively fit: its semi-major axis a and flattening f were calibrated to global satellite measurements, not derived from first-principles geometry. So calling the dome's parameter-fit curve-fitting while WGS84 gets credit for geodesy is a double standard. The honest reading is that both models are empirical fits with different architectures; the dome's 6.2% RMSE on six routes is bad in absolute terms but is the kind of starting point any new geometric model has before further refinement. The reviewers should compare V13 to early geodetic models (Clarke 1866, Bessel 1841) which had similar errors before iterative refinement produced WGS84.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Add one paragraph to detail_verdict_text that explicitly addresses the WGS84-was-also-fit symmetry argument: 'A defender will note that WGS84 a and f are also fit to data, so calling the dome's 14,105 km curve-fitting is asymmetric. Two responses: (1) WGS84 was fit once to a global dataset and predicts billions of out-of-sample points at sub-cm accuracy with no exclusion zone — the dome's 14,105 km is fit to six points and validated only on those same six points; this is the in-sample/out-of-sample asymmetry, not an architectural complaint about parameter-fitting per se. (2) Early geodetic models (Clarke 1866, Bessel 1841) reached <0.5% relative error on continental baselines — the dome's V13 reaches 6.2% on six chosen routes and excludes a substantial fraction of the surface. Iterative refinement is fine; the dome is iteratively refining away from its own first-principles architecture toward globe data, which is the circularity flagged in Kill-Shot #1.' This preempts the strongest fair-minded defense without lengthening the rest of the verdict.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-027/057/069 share detail_group and topic (disc-vs-globe coordinate fitting). code_analysis.derives_from_dome differs across them: WIN-027=false, WIN-057=true (no evidence field), WIN-069=true (with explicit derives_from_dome_evidence noting the parameter is fit to globe data). The bare 'true' in WIN-057 without WIN-069's nuance, while WIN-027 has 'false' on a structurally similar quadratic-fit case, is a tag-consistency problem within the detail_group. WIN-056 also uses the same 'derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters' post-hoc template phrasing and is in a related family — re-reviewing the post-hoc rider on WIN-056 against its own evidence is recommended once WIN-057 is fixed (could be the same template-misapplication bug). Drift audit was generated 2026-05-05T20:52Z; if WIN-056's post-hoc rider was added by the same edit that produced WIN-057's c2→c3 drift, the same hole likely repeats there.

claims_checked: 6.2% RMSE is ~600× worse than WGS84/Vincenty sub-0.01%
Dome uses 4+ fitted parameters vs WGS84's 2 (a, f)
40% of world population within ~12° of equator (OPEN-015 exclusion zone)
quantitative_verification.computations: Ratio 6.2/0.01 = 620, so '600×' claim verified within rounding. Counted parameters from detail_evidence: H₀, λ, EW angular scale 0.9941, equatorial ring radius 14,105 km — that is 4 explicit parameters, claim '4+' verified. WGS84 a=6,378,137 m and f=1/298.257 — 2 parameters, verified. Population claim: rough integration over gridded world population datasets (Kummu & Varis 2011 / SEDAC GPW family) gives ~17-22% of world population within ±12° latitude band; the full ±23.5° tropical band is the figure that lands at ~40%. Claim '40% within 12°' appears to conflate the narrower band with the tropics.
quantitative_verification.discrepancies: '40% of the world's population' within ~12° of equator overstates the exclusion zone population by roughly 2× — the correct figure for ±12° is ~17-22%, while ~40% is the tropical-zone (±23.5°) figure. See holes_found moderate item #3.

deeper_analysis: The c2→c3 drift on WIN-057 is the addition of a generic post-hoc-test rider in detail_verdict_text. The rider is part of a five-WIN family using the exact phrase 'derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters' (WIN-012, WIN-030, WIN-056, WIN-057, WIN-069 — counted across data/wins.json by raw-string search). For most of those, the claim's underlying mechanism is plausibly dome-derived. For WIN-057 specifically, the mechanism is fitted (the 14,105 km equatorial ring radius is fit to data, the V13 topology is the framework). The template was applied without checking whether 'derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters' is the correct framing for this specific WIN — and the result is a within-WIN self-contradiction with detail_evidence. This is the kind of hole a defender targets: not on the underlying scientific argument (which is solid) but on internal-consistency exploits where a quote-mine produces an apparent contradiction. The verdict (Misleading) holds; the action is rephrasing the rider, not changing the verdict. Recommended action minor_edit. Note: this re-review was produced under degraded data conditions — wins.json on main is currently unparseable as JSON due to a separate bug introduced in commit d09d9780 (workspace-sync 2026-05-08T02:05:18Z) affecting WIN-064's detail_extra. WIN-057 content was read from the last-good commit 6e68bba7 (2026-05-07T14:05:16Z) where the file parses cleanly; the content matches main on git diff. A separate critical alert covering the wins.json corruption is being filed alongside this review (INFRA-WINS-JSON-PARSE-2026-05-08).

citations_verified: Vincenty 1975 doi:10.1179/sre.1975.23.176.88 (Survey Review) — DOI shape valid; Vincenty's geodetic inverse formulae paper is the canonical sub-mm WGS84 reference.
OPEN-015 dome-side reference — dome author's own published open problem confirming <12° exclusion.
Internal anchor #posthoc-standard — 1 occurrence in data/sections.json (Part 3.1b), anchor exists.
citations_unchecked: https://john09289.github.io/predictions (dome author's site definition page) — content reachable; existence of 'dome author's own definition' under that URL not byte-checked this run. Suggest poller include this URL in its periodic check if not already.
